next_action.note: G32 complete: mined a cited, anti-fabrication shortlist of 5 verified standalone-codec backtest candidates with real fix+parent SHAs; top 3 scored as fixtures/fit-cases/*.fit (wabt-leb128-u64 8/8, capnproto-base64 8/8, openssl-punycode 6/8 priority 12). G33 target recommended: wabt ReadU64Leb128, range 89582f5 -> f1f3d6d, silent too-big-u64 LEB128 acceptance caught by round_trip, chosen over higher-priority punycode (OOB write a crash catches, scored down on silent_survival). Next G33: vendor wabt ReadU64Leb128 behind the trusted driver, mint per-revision .ngb over the fix range, add per-case CASE.md provenance + root THIRD-PARTY.md attribution, run scripts/backtest-relation.sh, property pre-registered.

history.note: ADR-001 initial Continue (infrastructure)
ADR-001 Continue scoped to verifiable editing; integrity test ngb 3/3 vs elf 0/3; Python removed from eval
Deterministic two-agent loop; 2 rounds (reject wrong stdout + accept); gated in check-all-proofs
Ground-truth conformance floor; conf-eval computes expected from spec independently; accept add_two (exit 2) reject add_two_chain (exit 4) vs add(1,1); gated in check-all-proofs
RODATA_BYTE_WRITE micro-op; static accept rodata off 152, reject code off 135; gated in check-all-proofs
Computed oracle; conf-eval yield=stdout; two-agent loop derives want-stdout from add(21,22), no literal 43
Value-bound micro-op; ngb-microop --expect-new from conf-eval digit; reject new=34 value_mismatch, accept new=33; static, no execution
Live-agent harness + first run; Cursor CLI composer-2.5; stacked 1r/34s, auditor-only 1r/42s; skill leaked answer, retry claim untested
Blind live falsification; skill leak removed but answer still in ~18 repo files and no tool-call trace, so inconclusive; retry-count framing dropped as wrong claim
Operational-error matrix; deterministic; static gate rejects 4/4 bad classes pre-execution with --expect-off + --expect-new; gated in check-all-proofs
Isolated author sandbox; prepare-author-sandbox + audit-author-isolation; live loop --workspace SANDBOX; check-author-sandbox in check-all-proofs
CA conformance; conf-eval op=eca; phase1 popcount invariant + rule30 golden; phase2 two Route B variants accept + wrong-rule reject; gated in check-all-proofs
CA hardening; ca_rule30_patched one-byte negative; check-linux-runner; CA author sandbox + scripted loop gated; ruliad exploration ledger
CA rules 50 and 110; ca_eca.c shared source; golden oracle + conformance for 30/50/110 in check-all-proofs

Honest CA ledger (dropped periodic/universal-class overclaims); conf-eval init=right; ca_eca.c parameterized; rule110 re-minted width 96/gens 96/init=right (9.3 KB golden, fills grid); gates unchanged, all-proofs green
Input-bound math conformance; conf-eval op=gcd input=argv; gcd.c freestanding argv specimen; oracle + conformance gates; v1/v2 accept all cases, wrong rejects; check-all-proofs green

Near-miss negative; a+b far-miss replaced by Euclid-loop-to-if (right only when b divides a); gate asserts near-miss accepts >=1 and rejects >=1; proves multi-case sampling has teeth; all-proofs green

Adversarial verifier vs static sampling; competition with independent oracle (conf-eval referee); gcd_evil (equal operands -> 1) passes G21/G22 static suite, searcher rejects with witness (2,2), v1/v2 accept; batched runner + isolated-rerun witness confirm; gated in check-all-proofs

Metamorphic involution verification, oracle-free; VerificationRequest seam (bswap32.req relation=involution entry=argv domain=u32 eq=exact); metamorphic-verify composes f(f(x)) over a u32 sweep in two batched passes, no expected value; honest bswap32 accepts, rotl8 rejects with witness x=1, involution-but-wrong outer-swap imposter accepts (asserted ceiling); complements value-oracle floor; gated in check-all-proofs

Closed the involution ceiling; conf-eval op=bswap (single argv operand, u32 decimal); bswap32.spec + bswap32.cases hand table; check-bswap-value-oracle runs both floors on the same bswap32_imposter: involution relation accepts (G24 ceiling), value oracle rejects with witness x=256 (got=256 want=65536); cheap-then-expensive handoff demonstrated on one artifact; gated in check-all-proofs

Both floors verify real vendored upstream code; reverse32.c ships public-domain Bit Twiddling Hacks bit reversal verbatim behind a trusted driver; conf-eval op=bitrev independent loop reference; relation accepts real bit reversal, rejects EVIL_REVERSE mask typo with witness x=1; value oracle accepts real bytes, rejects bswap32 (involution-not-bitrev) with witness x=1; provenance asserted; gated in check-all-proofs

round_trip relation + first demo case on a UTF-8 codec; utf8.c enc/dec packs bytes as 0x01++bytes into one operand; honest decoder rejects overlong/surrogate/range, OVERLONG_OK accepts the classic C0 80 -> U+0000 hole; fixed canonical unit test decode(encode(cp))==cp stays green on the buggy binary, round_trip (encode(decode(b))==b over a byte domain incl overlong) rejects it with witness bytes=114816 hex=C080 decode=0 reencode=256; ADR-010 adds the scope note (execution-grounded + byte-integrity, not C-only) and parks the whitepaper; gated in check-all-proofs

Packaged G27 as scripts/demo-utf8.sh (narrated contrast: overlong slip in source, canonical unit test green on the buggy binary, round_trip rejecting with witness C0 80, honest binary accepting; reuses committed fixtures) and wrote docs/ICP.md (one ICP: codec/serializer/parser maintainers; oracle problem, silent security bugs, false confidence, invariant-native; non-customers and confirm/kill signal named). Positioning only, no floor or format change

Case-fit rubric as a runnable target score; scripts/score-case-fit.sh gates a fixtures/fit-cases/*.fit scorecard on four fit factors all nonzero at once (oracle_hardness, property_checkable, observable, silent_survival), fit_score 0-8, priority = fit_score * criticality (separate axis); docs/CASE-FIT-RUBRIC.md documents levels + worked examples; check-case-fit-rubric.sh asserts payment-conservation FIT priority 16 and robotics-control-loop NOT-A-FIT (observable=0) plus malformed-input poka-yoke; gated in check-all-proofs; positioning/selection tooling, no floor or format change

Promoted the backtest spike into a committed harness; scripts/mint-one-elf.sh factors per-file docker build+pack; scripts/mint-backtest-utf8.sh derives three UTF-8 codec revisions (honest/overlong-bug/fix) and mints committed .ngb; scripts/backtest-relation.sh is a relation-agnostic driver over a timeline.manifest; scripts/check-backtest-utf8.sh asserts accept -> reject (witness C0 80) -> accept and fix-returns-to-rev1-hash; runner-guarded, gated in check-all-proofs; docs/BACKTEST.md documents method + phase-2 reuse; no floor or format change

Second worked backtest case (LEB128 varint) proves the driver generalizes; fixtures/metamorphic/leb128.c + leb128.req, gen_leb128 the only new verifier code, unchanged round_trip catches non-minimal acceptance (80 00 -> 0, witness 8000); generalized G30 mint/gate to scripts/mint-backtest.sh + scripts/check-backtest.sh (deleted utf8-specific), check-all-proofs runs both (utf8 C080, leb128 8000); docs/BACKTEST.md adds the case + formal-mining plan; METAMORPHIC-RELATIONS round_trip marked implemented; no floor or format change

Mined a cited shortlist of 5 verified standalone-codec backtest candidates (real fix+parent SHAs, no fabrication); top 3 as fixtures/fit-cases/*.fit (wabt-leb128-u64 8/8, capnproto-base64 8/8, openssl-punycode 6/8 pri 12); recommended G33 = wabt ReadU64Leb128 (89582f5 -> f1f3d6d) over higher-priority punycode because punycode is an OOB write a crash catches, not a silent relation violation; shortlist-only, no floor or format change
